neural_search_development_instructionset.mission: Convert Neural Search from a research/demo prototype into a trustworthy, reproducible, evidence-first neuroscience dataset discovery tool. Prioritize correctness, data quality, exact lookup reliability, provenance, evaluation validity, and product clarity over adding new speculative features.

operating_principles.principle: Do not expand the architecture before hardening the runtime path.
Every claim must map to a versioned artifact, benchmark, test, or source file.
Exact dataset accession lookup must be deterministic and should bypass normal ranking.
Expose uncertainty and missing metadata as first-class product information.
Keep demo, real-corpus, paper, and generated artifacts clearly separated.
Prefer small verified vertical slices over broad half-wired features.

ecc_usage.recommendation: Use ECC for verification loops, Python/testing rules, TypeScript/frontend rules, security scanning, and structured handoffs.

rules_to_avoid_initially.rule_set: Do not copy every ECC rule pack. Keep Claude context focused on this Python/FastAPI/React project.
Do not duplicate plugin-installed hooks or skills through a second full installer.

phase_0_repo_hygiene.goal: Make the repo clean enough that future agent work does not drown in generated artifacts.

tasks.task: Create scripts/export_clean_repo.sh or improve the existing one so exported zips exclude .git, node_modules, __pycache__, .pytest_cache, raw large files, and stale generated artifacts.
Move stale plans/reports into docs/archive or reports/archive. Keep docs/ACTIVE_ROADMAP.md as the single current roadmap.
Add make doctor to check Python version, package install, Node/npm, expected corpus files, graph file, embedding index, and Docker readiness.
Fix infra/Dockerfile.api. It currently copies packages/, but the repo uses neural_search/. Ensure Docker builds from a clean checkout.

acceptance_tests.test: make doctor passes in a fresh clone.
docker compose build api succeeds.
clean export zip contains no .git, node_modules, pycache, or pytest cache.

phase_1_corpus_snapshot_runtime.goal: Replace demo-only runtime with a versioned real-corpus runtime.

tasks.task: Implement CorpusSnapshotV1 with datasets, papers, graph, embedding index metadata, QA state, source files, counts, checksums, and created_at.
Add neural_search/corpus/snapshot.py and neural_search/corpus/runtime.py.
Add config/runtime.yaml with mode: demo | real, active_snapshot_path, graph_path, embeddings_path, qa_state_path.
Refactor apps/api/main.py to load CorpusSnapshotV1 during lifespan instead of build_demo_seed() when mode=real.
Expose active snapshot metadata in /healthz and /api/corpus/readiness.

acceptance_tests.test: /healthz returns active mode, snapshot id, dataset_count, paper_count, graph_node_count, graph_edge_count.
/api/search uses the real corpus when runtime mode is real.
Demo mode remains available but visually and programmatically marked as demo.

tasks.task: Create neural_search/search/exact_lookup.py.
Parse identifiers including DANDI:000026, dandi 000026, dataset:dandi:000026, OpenNeuro ds003505, ds003505, and source_id variants.
Before normal ranking, pin exact matches to rank 1 unless an explicit hard negative excludes them.
Add why_matched evidence: Exact accession match, Source alias match, Dataset ID match.
Add regression tests for v11 direct lookup failures.

acceptance_tests.test: rc_lookup_001 returns dataset:dandi:000026 at rank 1.
rc_lookup_002 returns dataset:dandi:000020 at rank 1.
rc_lookup_003 returns dataset:openneuro:ds003505 at rank 1.
Exact lookup still respects explicit NOT constraints.

phase_3_metadata_quality_sprint.goal: Increase useful field coverage for the 738-record real corpus, starting with DANDI and OpenNeuro.

tasks.task: Create corpus completeness report by source and field.
Improve DANDI extraction for species, modalities, brain_regions, tasks, data_standards, file_formats, assets, and related publications.
Improve OpenNeuro/BIDS extraction for participants, modality directories, task labels, events.tsv presence, dataset_description, file formats, and license.
Every extracted label must include confidence, source_field, source_value, evidence_text, extractor_name, and extractor_version.
Add a low-confidence review queue sorted by search impact and missingness.

acceptance_tests.test: Coverage report generated at reports/corpus_completeness_v1.md and .json.
No critical field has 0% coverage unless documented as unavailable by source.
DANDI/OpenNeuro core field coverage improves relative to the current baseline.

phase_4_affordance_population_and_valida.goal: Populate and validate analysis_affordances for real dataset records.

tasks.task: Run metadata-based affordance detector across all real normalized datasets.
Persist analysis_affordances back into normalized records or derived enriched records.
For at least 50 NWB/BIDS datasets, inspect actual file structure/content using existing validators.
Compute precision/recall against content-derived labels and human review where available.
Show affordance support/missingness in API and frontend result cards.

acceptance_tests.test: analysis_affordances coverage is non-zero and source-specific.
Affordance validation report includes precision/recall and failure examples.
UI displays supported, partial, unsupported, and unknown affordances separately.

phase_5_embedding_index_manifest.goal: Make dense retrieval reproducible and debuggable.

tasks.task: Create EmbeddingIndexManifest schema.
Track model name, model version, provider, dimension, normalize flag, corpus snapshot id, embedding file checksum, ANN backend, bit width, and generated_at.
Fail loudly or warn clearly if query provider cannot match corpus embedding provider.
Run and report lexical, hashing, BGE, BGE+graph, BGE+graph+affordance benchmark ladder.

acceptance_tests.test: Embedding index cannot silently mismatch dimensions or provider names.
Benchmark ladder report can be regenerated from make evaluation-suite.
API includes retrieval warnings if dense provider falls back or is disabled.

phase_6_evidence_first_ui.goal: Make the interface elegant, transparent, and scientifically useful.

tasks.task: Redesign DatasetCard component around evidence: match badges, score breakdown, missing metadata, affordance matrix, linked papers, and suggested first analyses.
Add source/id badges and one-click copyable accession IDs.
Add filters/facets for source, species, modality, brain region, task, data standard, QA status, and affordance.
Add search trace drawer: parsed query, exact lookup lane, constraints, score components, graph evidence, embedding evidence, warnings.
Add comparison view that emphasizes compatibility and complementarity, not just metadata side-by-side.

acceptance_tests.test: A scientist can understand why each result is present without reading raw JSON.
Missing metadata is visible, not hidden.
Every score badge links to evidence or explanation.

tasks.task: Create benchmark_v1 with at least 100 queries across lookup, task, modality, species, region, affordance, cross-dataset comparison, and hard negatives.
Create annotation protocol for at least two annotators on a representative subset.
Compute inter-annotator agreement and adjudication notes.
Create docs/CLAIM_LEDGER.md with claim, status, evidence artifact, metric, limitation, and paper section.
Update whitepaper tables directly from generated JSON reports.

acceptance_tests.test: No headline metric in the paper is manually copied without a source artifact.
Demo metrics and real-corpus metrics are separated.
All future work claims are clearly labeled as future work.

neural_search_development_instructionset.first_claude_prompt: You are working in the Neural Search repository. Use ECC verification habits and keep changes small, tested, and evidence-backed. Do not add new broad features. First inspect the repository state, then implement the exact lookup lane and corpus snapshot runtime path. The acceptance target is that direct DANDI/OpenNeuro accession queries return exact expected datasets at rank 1, and the API can run against the real normalized corpus rather than only build_demo_seed(). Keep demo mode explicit. Add tests before or alongside implementation. Update the claim ledger and active roadmap when complete.
